description: A recurring weekly product or menu item promotion featuring limited-time offerings designed to create anticipation and drive weekly customer engagement through exclusivity and scarcity.
structural_recipe: Post a featured weekly item on a consistent day (typically Friday for Saudi market). Use celebratory language and emojis to build excitement. Include the product name, special offer framing, and direct call-to-action encouraging immediate participation or orders. Mix Arabic and English captions to broaden appeal. Tag relevant business category hashtags. Keep visual focus on the featured item with high production quality.
why_it_works: Weekly specials leverage the Saudi cultural emphasis on Friday as a social and family gathering day. The exclusivity creates urgency and FOMO (fear of missing out), encouraging immediate engagement. Recurring patterns build habit and predictability, training followers to check-in on specific days. The mystery or novelty element ('Mystery Cookie') drives curiosity-driven engagement.
transplantation_caveats: Timing must align with Saudi weekly rhythm (Friday primacy) to maximize relevance
Bilingual captions required to serve diverse Saudi demographic segments
Weekly consistency is critical—irregular posting breaks the pattern expectation and reduces effectiveness
Product must genuinely feel limited/exclusive or audience will perceive as inauthentic marketing
cultural_constraints: Friday timing optimization essential due to cultural significance
Bilingual execution (Arabic/English) reflects Saudi digital audience composition
